source_pr.title: executor: make CI function TestInvalidReadCacheTable more stable
source_pr.body:

2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #32825 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Just like https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/32959/
Try to make the function stable and remove it from the unstable list.

### Check List

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Side effects

Documentation

```release-note

```

symptoms: Try to make the function stable and remove it from the unstable list.
failure_signature: Try to make the function stable and remove it from the unstable list.
root_cause_explanation: The test depends on time-based behavior (`now(6)` / `AS OF TIMESTAMP`) and used an extremely small sleep window, which is brittle under CI timing jitter or coarse clock resolution. This PR stabilizes the test by adding a larger timing buffer (e.g., `time.Sleep(100ms)`) around the timestamp capture and by using query assertions that fail fast when unexpected errors occur.
fix_pattern: Avoid microsecond-scale sleeps in time-sensitive tests; add a sufficient timing buffer around `now()`/stale-read (`AS OF TIMESTAMP`) boundaries to make eventual/clock-dependent behavior stable in CI.

evidence: evidence: Try to make the function stable and remove it from the unstable list.
